title: sc_set_time_resolution() limitations
body:
Scenario:

Verilog model was verilated with `--timescale 1ns/10ps` flag.

The same time resolution specified in SystemC
```c++
int sc_main(int argc, char* argv[]) {
  sc_set_time_resolution(10, SC_PS);
  ...
```

While running getting this error:
```bash
%Error: SystemC's sc_set_time_resolution is 10^-99, which does not match Verilog timeprecision 10^-11. Suggest use 'sc_set_time_resolution(1s)', or Verilator '--timescale-override 1s/1s'
```

Looking at the code here.
https://github.com/verilator/verilator/blob/master/include/verilated.h#L1044

Looks like Verilator don't support 10 or 100 time units as Verilog or SystemC. Only 1.
